name: Costa Quebrada Coastal Trail
name_original: Playas y acantilados de Liencres – running por la Costa Quebrada
description: Jog this 4.7-mile (7.5 km) moderate route along the stunning cliffs and beaches of Liencres, part of the Costa Quebrada.
region_intro: Jogging around Santa Cruz De Bezana offers diverse natural landscapes and varied terrain along the northern coast of Spain in Cantabria. The region is characterized by dramatic coastal paths, unique rock formations, and a mix of sandy beaches and wooded areas. These features provide a range of running experiences suitable for different fitness levels, from gentle beach runs to more challenging trails with elevation. Best jogging routes around Santa Cruz De Bezana The most popular jogging route is Costa Quebrada Coastal Trail, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes about 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the "Broken Coast" with its stunning cliffs and unique rock formations. Another top favourite among local runners is Urros of Liencres – Covachos Tombolo loop from Cementerio de Ciriego, a difficult 10.1 miles (16.3 km) path. This route features impressive marine rock formations and the unique tombolo of Covachos Beach. Local runners also love the Covachos Beach 1 – Covachos Tombolo loop from Liencres, an 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail leading through coastal scenery and past the distinctive tombolo, often completed in about 1 hour 39 minutes. Jogging around Santa Cruz De Bezana is defined by dramatic coastal paths, diverse beaches, and the unique rock formations of Costa Quebrada. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy beach runs to more challenging trails with varied terrain. The routes in Santa Cruz De Bezana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.98 stars from over 18 reviews. More than 360 runners have used komoot to explore Santa Cruz De Bezana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Santa Cruz De Bezana?
A: There are over 35 running routes around Santa Cruz De Bezana, offering a diverse range of experiences for all fitness levels. These include coastal paths, beach runs, and trails through wooded areas.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Santa Cruz De Bezana?
A: The terrain around Santa Cruz De Bezana is quite varied, ranging from dramatic coastal paths with stunning cliffs and unique rock formations (like the Costa Quebrada) to sandy beaches perfect for a relaxed run. You'll also find routes through wooded hills and areas with mobile dunes, offering a mix of surfaces and elevations.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Santa Cruz De Bezana?
A: Yes, Santa Cruz De Bezana offers a few easier running routes, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed pace. These routes often feature gentler inclines and less technical terrain, such as sections along the beaches or more established paths.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Santa Cruz De Bezana?
A: The running routes in Santa Cruz De Bezana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.98 stars from over 18 reviews. Runners frequently praise the stunning coastal scenery, the variety of terrain, and the opportunity to explore unique natural features like the rock formations of Costa Quebrada and the tombolo at Covachos Beach.

Q: Are there any challenging long-distance running trails in the area?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, Santa Cruz De Bezana offers several difficult long-distance running trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14996158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urros of Liencres – Covachos Tombolo loop from Cementerio de Ciriego&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.1-mile (16.3 km) path featuring significant elevation changes and impressive marine rock formations. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14996220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urros of Liencres – Canallave Beach loop from Soto de la Marina&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 13 miles (21.1 km).

Q: Can I find running routes with scenic coastal views or unique natural features?
A: Yes, Santa Cruz De Bezana is renowned for its breathtaking coastal scenery. Many running routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999084067/costa-quebrada-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Costa Quebrada Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offer stunning views of cliffs and unique rock formations. You can also run along beautiful beaches like Playa de Covachos, known for its tombolo, or explore the impressive Urros de Liencres marine rock formations.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or viewpoints to look out for on the running routes?
A: Yes, the area offers several interesting landmarks and viewpoints. You might encounter the unique tombolo at Playa de Covachos, only visible at low tide. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3927126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Tolío&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3691513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Picota&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286774" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Marisma del Conde&lt;/a&gt; also offers picturesque vistas.

Q: Are there running routes that pass through natural parks or protected areas?
A: While directly within Santa Cruz de Bezana, the Dunas de Liencres Natural Park, though technically in a neighboring municipality, is a significant natural area nearby. It features mobile dunes and pine forests, offering a distinct natural environment for exploration and running, and can be incorporated into longer routes originating from Santa Cruz de Bezana.

Q: Are there family-friendly running trails in Santa Cruz De Bezana?
A: Many of the less challenging coastal paths and beach runs in Santa Cruz De Bezana are suitable for families. These routes often provide open spaces and beautiful scenery, making them enjoyable for all ages. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's fitness level.

Q: Are the running routes in Santa Cruz De Bezana dog-friendly?
A: Generally, many outdoor trails in the region are dog-friendly, especially those in natural areas. However, specific regulations regarding dogs (e.g., leash requirements) can vary, particularly on beaches or in protected zones. It's always best to check local signage or official guidelines for the specific route you plan to run with your dog.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Santa Cruz De Bezana?
A: Santa Cruz De Bezana benefits from a mild climate influenced by Atlantic Ocean winds, making it suitable for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, but coastal breezes often provide relief, and early mornings or late evenings are ideal. Winter runs are also possible, though you might encounter more rain.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Santa Cruz De Bezana?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Santa Cruz De Bezana are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14996158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urros of Liencres – Covachos Tombolo loop from Cementerio de Ciriego&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14996340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covachos Beach 1 – Covachos Tombolo loop from Liencres&lt;/a&gt;, which offer convenient circular options for your run.

Q: Is parking available near the running trailheads?
A: Yes, for many popular running routes and coastal access points in and around Santa Cruz De Bezana, you will find designated parking areas. For instance, routes starting near beaches like Playa de San Juan de la Canal or access points to the Costa Quebrada often have parking facilities nearby. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information.
tips: Through the offshore island, the waves burn from 2 sides on the beach here.

The urros of Liencres will not go unnoticed before your eyes and offer a different touch to the landscape of the Cantabrian coast. These are folds and rocky ledges that protrude from the coast as if they were islands.

Arnía beach is located in the natural park of the Dunas de Liencres and Costa Quebrada, between the municipalities of Piélagos and Santa Cruz de Bezana, in the autonomous community of Cantabria, Spain. The beach is located in a rocky environment, with rocks that are more than 90 million years old.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this route?
faqs.answer: This jogging route, covering approximately 7.5 kilometers, generally takes around 55 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the stunning coastal views.

faqs.question: Is this jogging trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the overall route is rated as 'moderate', sections of the trail are easily accessible and suitable for all skill levels. Other parts require good fitness and sure-footedness, especially near cliffs or on potentially slippery paths. Beginners should be mindful of these more challenging sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a dynamic mix of terrain. You'll jog along dramatic cliffs, past unique rock formations like the 'urros' and 'flysch' at Arnía Beach, and through pine forests within the Liencres Natural Park. Expect varied surfaces, from compacted earth to rocky sections, with constant erosion by the Cantabrian Sea shaping the path.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the jogging route?
faqs.answer: As you jog, you'll encounter spectacular geological formations and beautiful beaches. Key highlights include the unique Covachos Tombolo, the impressive Urros of Liencres, and the scenic Arnía Beach. The entire Costa Quebrada is a UNESCO Global Geopark, offering a fascinating 'open-air geology lesson' with informational panels along the way.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog along the Costa Quebrada?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds, making them ideal. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer. Winter jogging is possible, but be aware that paths can be muddy and slippery, and strong winds or high seas might affect conditions near the cliffs.

faqs.question: Is the Playas y acantilados de Liencres route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the trails within the Parque Natural de las Dunas de Liencres y Costa Quebrada. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the proximity to cliffs, potential wildlife, and other trail users. Please remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the Parque Natural de las Dunas de Liencres y Costa Quebrada?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Parque Natural de las Dunas de Liencres y Costa Quebrada. It is a publicly accessible natural park, but visitors are expected to respect the natural environment and follow park regulations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the jogging trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is available at various points along the Costa Quebrada, particularly near popular beaches like Arnía or Valdearenas. Depending on your starting point, you can find designated parking areas that provide direct access to the coastal paths. Arriving earlier in the day, especially on weekends, can help secure a spot.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this jogging route largely follows sections of the Senda del Litoral, which is a coastal path extending along the Cantabrian coastline. You may also find it briefly intersects with local paths like those around Barrio La Arnía.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety precautions I should take while jogging near the cliffs?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The Costa Quebrada features dramatic cliffs, and caution is advised. Stay on marked paths and maintain a safe distance from cliff edges, especially in windy conditions or after rain when paths can be slippery. Be aware of natural blowholes ('bufones') where water can emerge forcefully during high seas.

faqs.question: Can I find food or drink options near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is natural, you'll find small cafes and restaurants in the nearby villages, particularly around Liencres and the more developed beach areas like Arnía. These can be good spots for a post-jog refreshment or meal.
